title: Supreme Court decision could deliver GOP a host of House seats in 2028
description: While it’s too late for many states to redraw congressional maps for the 2026 midterms, the court’s ruling to limit the Voting Rights Act could result in fewer Black members. “We’ve been dealt a bad hand,” says Georgia lawmaker.

title: Trump, Iran are locked in high-stakes standoff as oil prices hit wartime high
description: The U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones continue to halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz as each side bets the other will break first.

title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
description: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.

title: After lackluster tour turnout, Turning Point wraps things up with excited crowd in Idaho
description: The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.

title: Florida lawmakers pass voting map that could help Republicans flip 4 House seats
description: Florida lawmakers passed a new voting map that could give Republicans an edge in flipping four House seats now held by Democrats. It aids President Trump's national redistricting push.

title: Louisiana governor prepares to suspend House primaries after court ruling
description: Gov. Jeff Landry (R) told Republican House candidates he plans to suspend the May 16 primary elections so lawmakers can redraw the congressional map.

title: On chaotic day, Johnson navigates multiple internal revolts in House
description: Over hours on and around the chamber’s floor, the speaker and fellow Republican leaders cajoled holdouts on a surveillance bill, a farm policy bill and funding for DHS.

title: Comey’s lawyers say they will challenge threat charges as vindictive
description: The former FBI director made his first court apperance after being indicted over a 2025 social media post that prosecutors say was a threat to the life of the president.

title: Jury convicts Afghan man of terrorism, doesn’t link him to deaths at Kabul airport
description: Jurors convict the man on a charge of conspiring to provide material support to an ISIS group, but it’s a rare setback in a high-profile terrorism case.

title: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
description: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

title: Hegseth spars with lawmakers as Iran war’s price tag hits $25 billion
description: In a fiery House meeting on the military budget, the defense secretary called lawmakers critical of the administration’s handling of the conflict “defeatist.”

title: What the Supreme Court did to the Voting Rights Act
description: Southern lawmakers can now draw congressional maps to break up minority voters, as the conservative majority continues to roll back decades-old civil rights protections.

title: U.S. aircraft carrier to leave Mideast, reducing military might amid Iran war
description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, at sea for 10 months, is in need of repair. Its exit, though, reduces the firepower on hand as Trump presses Tehran to make peace.

title: Supreme Court wrestles with Trump effort to end temporary protections for migrants
description: The conservative justices appeared sympathetic to the administration’s contention that it can cancel humanitarian protections for Haitian and Syrian nationals.

title: Trump administration moves to dismiss members of the National Science Board
description: The Trump administration has moved to dismiss the members of the National Science Board, which governs the National Science Foundation. Dismissed members say the agency's independence is threatened.

title: Trump’s $1 million ‘gold card’ visa has not been as popular as advertised
description: Just 165 people have paid the application fee so far, the government said in a new legal filing.

title: House extends a controversial spy tool, but Senate path is unclear ahead of deadline
description: The House has approved a three year extension of the surveillance program known as FISA Section 702. The bill now heads to the Senate, where it faces a difficult path to final passage.

title: Pam Bondi agrees to testify before Congress on Epstein files, committee says
description: The former attorney general had been subpoenaed to appear at a closed-door session. When she was fired by Trump, her appearance was thrown into limbo.

title: Florida lawmakers approve redistricting plan intended to give GOP four more seats
description: Republican state legislators drew new districts that are expected to be challenged in court.

title: Florida lawmakers pass a voting map that could help Republicans flip 4 House seats
description: The map drawn by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis boosts President Trump's effort to reshape voting before the midterm elections. The GOP likely holds a slight edge over Democrats in redistricting now.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Supreme Court says antiabortion center can fight subpoena for donors’ names
description: Advocacy groups across the political spectrum aligned with the faith-based center in its battle with New Jersey’s attorney general over disclosing its donors.

title: How Trump's EPA head has transformed the agency — and sided with polluters
description: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."

title: '8647' got James Comey indicted. What exactly does it mean?
description: A grand jury charged Comey with threatening Trump's life through his since-deleted 2025 post of seashells forming "8647." Trump is the 47th president, and the term "86" has a few possible meanings.

title: The U.S. Supreme Court strikes another severe blow to the Voting Rights Act
description: The court, in a 6-3 decision along partisan lines, ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map, which created a second majority-Black congressional district, was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander."

title: As Supreme Court weighs Trump's immigration policy, senior citizens join the fight
description: As the Supreme Court weighs the Trump administration's termination of Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians, seniors are advocating for protections for their immigrant caregivers.

title: Prosecutions of Trump’s foes add to GOP’s headaches in midterms
description: Charges against former FBI director James B. Comey and others take focus away from the economy and the issues voters care most about, many Republicans say.

title: Roots of political violence aren’t as simple as Trump makes them sound
description: Social upheaval, deep polarization and social media are primary drivers of the phenomenon, which has touched Republicans and Democrats alike.

title: Trump welcomes King Charles III and Queen Camilla for state dinner
description: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed Britain’s King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the White House for a state dinner in the East Room.

title: King Charles urges checks on executive power as Trump hosts royal visit
description: In a speech to Congress, the monarch avoided naming the president but highlighted democratic norms, Ukraine and Western unity.

title: James Comey indicted over 2025 social media post allegedly threatening Trump
description: The former FBI director shared a photo showing seashells arranged to write out “86 47,” which some saw as a call for violence against President Donald Trump.

title: Former Fauci aide charged in alleged effort to thwart pandemic inquiries
description: David M. Morens, who was charged in an indictment unsealed this week, has previously acknowledged efforts to shield his communications from federal public records laws.

title: Image of Trump to be featured inside new passports to mark America’s 250th
description: The president’s likeness — along with Declaration of Independence text and the American flag — will be on an inside page of at least some new passports.

title: DOJ cites attack, ‘Trump Derangement Syndrome’ in seeking ballroom approval
description: The legal filing, which resembles Trump’s Truth Social posts, comes as the president and his allies ramp up their efforts to complete the project.

title: GAO agrees to review Justice Department’s handling of Epstein files
description: The review by the Government Accountability Office, an independent agency that is part of the legislative branch, will look into how the department released the files.

title: A Supreme Court justice’s personal ties to Haiti highlight stakes in asylum case
description: The court is considering President Donald Trump’s effort to end temporary protected status for Haitians. Justice Amy Coney Barrett has two adopted children from Haiti.
